---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Qingyang Du"
date: "2026-02-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Adherence: Outcome ~ Hard drug + ADH + other Covariates for both frequentist and bayesian
Only descriptive for Hard drug ~ ADH


```{r}
###############################################################################
#                   FREQUENTIST ANALYSIS SCRIPT
#
# Project:  Treatment Response to HAART and Hard Drug Use
# Prereq:   Run 01_data_cleaning_v3.R, then 03_table1_kableExtra.R
#           (the latter saves hiv_complete_case.RData with dat_cc)
#
# Models (for each of the 4 outcomes):
#   Model 1: Unadjusted — Y2 outcome ~ hard_drugs_bl
#   Model 2: Adjusted   — Y2 outcome ~ hard_drugs_bl + baseline outcome
#                          + age + race + education + BMI + smoking
#   Model 3: Adjusted + Adherence — Model 2 + ADH_cat
#            (to assess whether adherence mediates the drug use effect)
#
# Confounders per investigator: age, race, education, BMI, smoking
# (all retained regardless of significance)
###############################################################################


# =============================================================================
# 0.  LOAD PACKAGES AND DATA
# =============================================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(broom)        # tidy() for model summaries
library(car)          # vif() for multicollinearity

load("hiv_cleaned.RData")   # dat_cc

# Verify structure
dim(dat_cc)
table(dat_cc$hard_drugs_bl, useNA = "always")


# =============================================================================
# 1.  MODEL SPECIFICATION
# =============================================================================
# Define formulas centrally so they are easy to modify.

# Outcome-specific baseline covariate
outcomes <- list(
  list(name  = "delta_log_VLOAD",
       y     = "delta_log_VLOAD",
       label = "Change in Log10 Viral Load"),
  list(name  = "delta_LEU3N",
       y     = "delta_LEU3N",
       label = "Change in CD4+ T Cell Count"),
  list(name  = "delta_AGG_PHYS",
       y     = "delta_AGG_PHYS",
       label = "Change in Physical QoL"),
  list(name  = "delta_AGG_MENT",
       y     = "delta_AGG_MENT",
       label = "Change in Mental QoL")
)

# Confounders (same for all outcomes)
confounders <- c("age_bl", "RACE_bin_bl", "EDUC_bin_bl", "BMI_bl", "SMOKE_bin_bl")

# Build formula strings
make_formula <- function(y, rhs_vars) {
  as.formula(paste(y, "~", paste(rhs_vars, collapse = " + ")))
}


# =============================================================================
# 2.  FIT MODELS
# =============================================================================

results_list <- list()

for (o in outcomes) {

  # Model 1: Unadjusted
f1 <- make_formula(o$y, "hard_drugs_bl")

# Model 2: Adjusted (no baseline outcome — it's in the change score)
f2 <- make_formula(o$y, c("hard_drugs_bl", confounders))

# Model 3: Adjusted + Adherence
f3 <- make_formula(o$y, c("hard_drugs_bl", confounders, "ADH_cat"))

  results_list[[o$name]] <- list(
    unadj    = m1,
    adj      = m2,
    adj_adh  = m3,
    label    = o$label,
    y        = o$y,
    y_bl     = o$y_bl
  )
}


# =============================================================================
# 3.  MODEL SUMMARIES
# =============================================================================

# --- 3.1 Full summaries for each model ---
for (nm in names(results_list)) {
  r <- results_list[[nm]]

  message("============================================================")
  message("OUTCOME: ", r$label)
  message("============================================================")

  message("\n--- Model 1: Unadjusted ---")
  print(summary(r$unadj))

  message("\n--- Model 2: Adjusted ---")
  print(summary(r$adj))

  message("\n--- Model 3: Adjusted + Adherence ---")
  print(summary(r$adj_adh))
}


# --- 3.2 Tidy coefficient table for the hard_drugs_bl effect ---
extract_drug_effect <- function(model, model_label, outcome_label) {
  tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    filter(term == "hard_drugs_blYes") %>%
    mutate(
      Outcome = outcome_label,
      Model   = model_label
    ) %>%
    select(Outcome, Model, estimate, std.error, statistic,
           p.value, conf.low, conf.high)
}

drug_effects <- map_dfr(names(results_list), function(nm) {
  r <- results_list[[nm]]
  bind_rows(
    extract_drug_effect(r$unadj,   "Unadjusted",            r$label),
    extract_drug_effect(r$adj,     "Adjusted",              r$label),
    extract_drug_effect(r$adj_adh, "Adjusted + Adherence",  r$label)
  )
})

# Round for readability
drug_effects <- drug_effects %>%
  mutate(across(c(estimate, std.error, statistic, conf.low, conf.high),
                ~ round(., 4)),
         p.value = round(p.value, 4))

drug_effects


# =============================================================================
# 4.  MEDIATION ASSESSMENT — COMPARE MODELS 2 AND 3
# =============================================================================
# If the hard_drugs_bl coefficient attenuates when adherence is added,
# this suggests adherence partially mediates the drug use effect.

mediation_compare <- drug_effects %>%
  filter(Model %in% c("Adjusted", "Adjusted + Adherence")) %>%
  select(Outcome, Model, estimate) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Model, values_from = estimate,
              names_prefix = "est_") %>%
  rename(est_adj     = `est_Adjusted`,
         est_adj_adh = `est_Adjusted + Adherence`) %>%
  mutate(
    change     = est_adj_adh - est_adj,
    pct_change = round(100 * change / est_adj, 1)
  )

mediation_compare


# =============================================================================
# 5.  MODEL DIAGNOSTICS
# =============================================================================

# --- 5.1  Residual diagnostic plots ---
# Generate 4-panel diagnostic plots for each adjusted model

for (nm in names(results_list)) {
  r <- results_list[[nm]]

  par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
  plot(r$adj, main = paste(r$label, "— Adjusted Model"))
  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}


# --- 5.2  Variance Inflation Factors (multicollinearity check) ---
vif_list <- map_dfr(names(results_list), function(nm) {
  r <- results_list[[nm]]
  vif_vals <- vif(r$adj)

  # vif() returns a matrix for factors; extract GVIF^(1/(2*Df))
  if (is.matrix(vif_vals)) {
    tibble(
      Outcome  = r$label,
      Variable = rownames(vif_vals),
      GVIF     = round(vif_vals[, "GVIF"], 3),
      Df       = vif_vals[, "Df"],
      GVIF_adj = round(vif_vals[, "GVIF^(1/(2*Df))"], 3)
    )
  } else {
    tibble(
      Outcome  = r$label,
      Variable = names(vif_vals),
      GVIF     = round(vif_vals, 3),
      Df       = 1,
      GVIF_adj = round(sqrt(vif_vals), 3)
    )
  }
})

vif_list


# --- 5.3  Normality of residuals (Shapiro-Wilk on a subsample) ---
# Full sample may be too large for shapiro.test; use random subsample of 500
set.seed(42)

normality_tests <- map_dfr(names(results_list), function(nm) {
  r <- results_list[[nm]]
  resids <- residuals(r$adj)
  n_samp <- min(length(resids), 500)
  samp   <- sample(resids, n_samp)
  sw     <- shapiro.test(samp)

  tibble(
    Outcome   = r$label,
    W         = round(sw$statistic, 4),
    p.value   = round(sw$p.value, 4),
    n_sampled = n_samp
  )
})

normality_tests


# --- 5.4  Residual plots by exposure group ---
# Check whether residual variance differs by hard drug use (homoscedasticity)

for (nm in names(results_list)) {
  r <- results_list[[nm]]

  diag_data <- dat_cc %>%
    filter(complete.cases(dat_cc[, c(r$y, r$y_bl, confounders)])) %>%
    mutate(
      fitted_vals = fitted(r$adj),
      residuals   = residuals(r$adj)
    )

  p <- ggplot(diag_data, aes(x = fitted_vals, y = residuals,
                              colour = hard_drugs_bl)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.7) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("No" = "#2C7BB6", "Yes" = "#D7191C"),
                        name = "BL Hard Drug Use") +
    labs(title = paste(r$label, "— Residuals vs. Fitted by Drug Use"),
         x = "Fitted Values", y = "Residuals") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")

  print(p)
}


# =============================================================================
# 6.  R-SQUARED COMPARISON
# =============================================================================

r2_table <- map_dfr(names(results_list), function(nm) {
  r <- results_list[[nm]]
  tibble(
    Outcome             = r$label,
    R2_unadj            = round(summary(r$unadj)$r.squared, 4),
    R2_adj_model        = round(summary(r$adj)$r.squared, 4),
    Adj_R2_adj_model    = round(summary(r$adj)$adj.r.squared, 4),
    R2_adj_adh          = round(summary(r$adj_adh)$r.squared, 4),
    Adj_R2_adj_adh      = round(summary(r$adj_adh)$adj.r.squared, 4)
  )
})

r2_table


# =============================================================================
# 7.  SUMMARY TABLE — HARD DRUG USE EFFECT ACROSS ALL MODELS
# =============================================================================

summary_table <- drug_effects %>%
  mutate(
    Effect = paste0(
      formatC(estimate, format = "f", digits = 3),
      " (",
      formatC(conf.low, format = "f", digits = 3),
      ", ",
      formatC(conf.high, format = "f", digits = 3),
      ")"
    ),
    p_display = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "<0.001",
                       formatC(p.value, format = "f", digits = 3))
  ) %>%
  select(Outcome, Model, Effect, p_display)

summary_table


# =============================================================================
# 8.  SAVE RESULTS
# =============================================================================

save(results_list, drug_effects, mediation_compare, r2_table,
     file = "freq_results.RData")


```
